title: Kth largest element in a stream of running integers

content.problemStatement:
Implement a class KthLargest to find the kth largest number in a stream. It should have the following methods: KthLargest(int k, int [] nums) Initializes the object with the integer k and the initial stream of numbers in nums
int add(int val) Appends the integer val to the stream and returns the kth largest element in the stream.
content.inputFormat: The input will be provided in the following format: The first line will contain two space-separated integers, k and n, where k is the kth largest element to find and n is the number of elements in the initial stream. The next line will contain n space-separated integers representing the initial stream of numbers. After that, each line will contain a single integer to be added to the stream.
content.outputFormat: For each integer added to the stream, output the kth largest element in the stream.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use a min-heap to store the k largest elements. When a new element is added, push it to the heap and pop the smallest element if the heap size exceeds k.
solution.timeComplexity: O(log k) for each add operation

code.Python3:
import heapq
class Solution:

    # Constructor initializes the heap with k largest elements
    def __init__(self, k: int, nums: list[int]):
        self.k = k

        # Initialize min-heap
        self.minHeap = []

        # Add elements to heap
        for num in nums:
            heapq.heappush(self.minHeap, num)

            # Remove smallest if heap exceeds k
            if len(self.minHeap) > k:
                heapq.heappop(self.minHeap)

    # Adds new value and returns the k-th largest
    def add(self, val: int) -> int:
        # Add new element
        heapq.heappush(self.minHeap, val)

        # Remove smallest if needed
        if len(self.minHeap) > self.k:
            heapq.heappop(self.minHeap)

        # Return root of heap
        return self.minHeap[0]

# Driver code
nums = [4, 5, 8, 2]
kthLargest = Solution(3, nums)
print(kthLargest.add(3))   # Output: 4
print(kthLargest.add(5))   # Output: 5
print(kthLargest.add(10))  # Output: 5
print(kthLargest.add(9))   # Output: 8
print(kthLargest.add(4))   # Output: 8
